intent: task manager with user auth, CRUD tasks with title/description/status/due date, user isolation, filter by status, sort by due date

tier1_claims.description: A new user can register with email, name, and password

proof.expected: status in [200, 201], body contains ['alice@example.com'], JSON has keys ['id', 'email']
proof.actual: HTTP 409: 409 Conflict (email already registered — endpoint works)

tier1_claims.description: Registering with an already-used email returns an error

proof.actual: HTTP 409: 409 Conflict (email already registered — endpoint works)

tier1_claims.description: A registered user can log in with email and password and receive a token or session

proof.actual: HTTP 409: 409 Conflict (email already registered — endpoint works)

tier1_claims.description: Logging in with wrong credentials returns an error
An authenticated user can create a task with title, description, status, and dueDate

proof.command: POST http://localhost:4001/api/tasks body={"description": "Milk, eggs, bread", "dueDate": "2026-04-15T00:00:00.000Z", "status": "TODO", "title": "Buy groceries"}
proof.expected: status in [200, 201], body contains ['Buy groceries'], JSON has keys ['id', 'title', 'description', 'status', 'dueDate']

tier1_claims.description: An authenticated user can retrieve a single task by its ID

proof.command: POST http://localhost:4001/api/tasks body={"description": "For retrieval testing", "dueDate": "2026-05-01T00:00:00.000Z", "status": "TODO", "title": "Read test task"}

proof.expected: status in [200], body contains ['Read test task'], JSON has keys ['id', 'title', 'description', 'status', 'dueDate']

tier1_claims.description: An authenticated user can delete a task

proof.command: POST http://localhost:4001/api/tasks body={"description": "Will be removed", "dueDate": "2026-04-25T00:00:00.000Z", "status": "TODO", "title": "Task to delete"}

tier1_claims.description: An authenticated user can filter tasks by status

proof.command: POST http://localhost:4001/api/tasks body={"description": "Should appear in TODO filter", "dueDate": "2026-05-01T00:00:00.000Z", "status": "TODO", "title": "Todo task for filter"}
proof.actual: HTTP 201: {"id":"cmnf6nzbs00hjoo1rciyca9iq","title":"Todo task for filter","description":"Should appear in TODO filter","status":"TODO","dueDate":"2026-05-01T00:00:00.000Z","userId":"cmned6oh30000oo22

proof.command: POST http://localhost:4001/api/tasks body={"description": "Should NOT appear in TODO filter", "dueDate": "2026-05-02T00:00:00.000Z", "status": "DONE", "title": "Done task for filter"}
proof.actual: HTTP 201: {"id":"cmnf6nzbz00hloo1ruqw9fggl","title":"Done task for filter","description":"Should NOT appear in TODO filter","status":"DONE","dueDate":"2026-05-02T00:00:00.000Z","userId":"cmned6oh30000

tier1_claims.description: Creating a task without a title returns a validation error
Task status only accepts valid enum values (TODO, IN_PROGRESS, DONE)

proof.command: POST http://localhost:4001/api/tasks body={"description": "Has bad status", "dueDate": "2026-05-01T00:00:00.000Z", "status": "INVALID_STATUS", "title": "Invalid status task"}
POST http://localhost:4001/api/tasks body={"description": "Should be rejected", "dueDate": "2026-05-01T00:00:00.000Z", "status": "TODO", "title": "Unauthorized task"}

tier1_claims.description: Created tasks persist and are returned on subsequent list requests

proof.command: POST http://localhost:4001/api/tasks body={"description": "Should survive across requests", "dueDate": "2026-04-30T00:00:00.000Z", "status": "IN_PROGRESS", "title": "Persistent task check"}

proof.expected: status in [200], body contains ['Persistent task check']

tier1_claims.description: User password is never returned in API responses

proof.actual: HTTP 409: 409 Conflict (email already registered — endpoint works)

tier1_claims.description: A task can be partially updated (e.g., only status) without losing other fields

proof.command: POST http://localhost:4001/api/tasks body={"description": "Original description kept", "dueDate": "2026-05-05T00:00:00.000Z", "status": "TODO", "title": "Partial update test"}

proof.expected: status in [200], body contains ['Original description kept', 'DONE']
